title: Alchemical Transmutation Forge
description: Inside a dungeon forge, the player performs multi-step alchemical transmutation on a car-paint-coated metallic sample. By combining elemental reagents in the correct sequence, adjusting temperature and pressure, and observing conservation of mass, the user transforms base metals into noble alloys while tracking undo/redo of each reaction step.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net). Load 'shared_assets/dungeon_warkarma.glb' as the environment — a dark dungeon forge scene. Load 'shared_assets/ClearCoatCarPaint.glb' as a metallic sample specimen placed on a stone pedestal at the center of the dungeon. Apply MeshPhysicalMaterial with clearcoat=1.0 to the car paint model to represent its reactive coating. Camera: PerspectiveCamera at (0,2,5) looking at origin, with OrbitControls limited to 60-degree polar range. Lighting: one warm PointLight (orange, intensity 3) simulating forge fire, one dim AmbientLight (0.15), and a blue RimLight for the specimen. UI panel (HTML overlay, left side): display current Mass (starts 100g, must be conserved across all steps), Temperature slider (300K-2000K), Pressure slider (1-50 atm), and a reagent selector with 5 elements (Sulfur, Mercury, Salt, Phosphorus, Antimony). The user must perform 10-14 interaction steps: (1) select a reagent, (2) adjust temperature, (3) adjust pressure, (4) click 'React' to apply — each reaction changes the specimen's clearcoat color/roughness/metalness based on reagent+temp+pressure combination. Enforce conservation of mass: adding a reagent adds its mass, product mass must equal input mass, shown numerically. If mass is violated the reaction is rejected with a red flash. Implement full undo/redo stack (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y and buttons) that reverts material properties, mass, and reagent history. Multi-input coupling: certain reagent pairs at specific temp+pressure ranges trigger special 'transmutation' effects (particle burst using Points geometry, specimen scale pulse animation). Track reaction history in a scrollable log panel (right side). After completing a valid 12-step sequence that cycles through all 5 reagents, display a golden glow completion effect on the specimen. All state changes animate smoothly over 0.5s using TWEEN or manual lerp in the render loop.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  currentMass: number,         // total mass in grams, must remain constant at 100g across all reactions
  temperature: number,         // forge temperature in Kelvin, range 300-2000
  pressure: number,         // atmospheric pressure in atm, range 1-50
  selectedReagent: string,         // currently selected reagent name (Sulfur|Mercury|Salt|Phosphorus|Antimony)
  reactionCount: number,         // total number of successful reactions performed
  clearcoatValue: number,         // current clearcoat intensity 0.0-1.0
  roughnessValue: number,         // current surface roughness 0.0-1.0
  metalnessValue: number,         // current metalness 0.0-1.0
  specimenColorHue: number,         // hue component of specimen color in degrees 0-360
  forgeFireIntensity: number,         // point light intensity representing forge heat
  massConservationViolations: number,         // count of mass conservation errors detected
  isReacting: boolean,         // true when a reaction is currently in progress
}
